# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 92

This is turn 92. Turns 1 through 91 are consolidated in `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 92.

## Turn 92 — terminal scalar branch is an exact image-poset equivalence

The candidate remains unchanged:

```text
sigma,tau,x0,x1

sigma2 = sigma^40491355905
u0 = (x0*tau)^40491355905
u1 = (x1*tau)^40491355905
d0 = u0*x0^-1
z0 = x0^sigma2
c0 = d0^-1*z0^-1*d0*z0

x0^2*x1^4*u1^-1*(x1^sigma)*c0
```

The tame relation is implicit. In the proof, the displayed exponent denotes the genuine profinite idempotent \(\omega _2\); \(40491355905\) is its required verifier residue. 

Turn 91 proved the terminal classification
\[
G\simeq H\times_{H_2}Q
\]
and the equivalence between surjectivity to \(G\) and surjectivity to \(Q\).  The same coprime-kernel argument determines every possible proper image, not only the full image.

### Terminal image-poset theorem

Let
\[
G=H\times_C Q,\qquad C=H_2,
\]
where
\[
\pi:H\twoheadrightarrow C,\qquad q:Q\twoheadrightarrow C,
\]
with
\[
N=\ker\pi\quad\text{of odd order},\qquad L=\ker q\quad\text{a \(2\)-group}.
\]

Then projection to \(Q\) gives an order-preserving bijection
\[
\left\{J\le G:\operatorname{pr}_H(J)=H\right\}
\longleftrightarrow
\left\{Q'\le Q:q(Q')=C\right\},
\tag{92.1}
\]
whose inverse is
\[
Q'\longmapsto J(Q'):=H\times_C Q'.
\tag{92.2}
\]

Indeed, take \(J\le G\) projecting onto \(H\), and put \(Q'=\operatorname{pr}_Q(J)\). Compatibility in the pullback gives \(q(Q')=C\). Now
\[
J\le H\times_C Q'
\]
projects surjectively onto both factors. The two relevant kernels are
\[
N=\ker(H\to C),\qquad L'=Q'\cap L=\ker(Q'\to C),
\]
whose orders are coprime. The Turn-91 subdirect lemma therefore gives
\[
J=H\times_C Q'.
\]

Conversely, every \(Q'\le Q\) with \(q(Q')=C\) plainly produces such a subgroup.

The correspondence also preserves the information required by the induction:
\[
[G:J(Q')]=[Q:Q'],
\tag{92.3}
\]
\[
J(Q')\cap L=\ker(Q'\to C),
\tag{92.4}
\]
and
\[
J(Q')\triangleleft G\quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad Q'\triangleleft Q.
\tag{92.5}
\]

Thus proper fixed-frame images in a terminal scalar target are exactly proper compatible subgroups of its \(2\)-group factor.

### Exact stratification of maps

Fix a tame epimorphism
\[
\alpha:T_{\mathrm{tame}}\twoheadrightarrow H
\]
and its induced map
\[
\alpha_2:\Pi\twoheadrightarrow C
\]
from the common marked maximal pro-\(2\) quotient
\[
\Pi=
\left\langle \sigma,x_0,x_1\ \middle|\ 
x_0^2x_1^3x_1^\sigma=1
\right\rangle_{\mathrm{pro}\text{-}2}.
\]

For either source
\[
\Gamma\in\{\Gamma_A,G_{\mathbb Q_2}\},
\]
a homomorphism \(f:\Gamma\to G\) inducing \(\alpha\) corresponds uniquely to
\[
\beta:\Pi\to Q,\qquad q\beta=\alpha_2.
\]

Put \(Q'=\operatorname{im}\beta\). The image \(J=\operatorname{im}f\) projects onto \(H\) and \(Q'\). Hence (92.1) gives the exact identity
\[
\operatorname{im}f
=
H\times_C\operatorname{im}\beta.
\tag{92.6}
\]

Consequently, for every compatible \(Q'\le Q\),
\[
\begin{aligned}
&\{f:\Gamma\to G:
f_{\mathrm{tame}}=\alpha,\ 
\operatorname{im}f=H\times_CQ'\}\\
&\qquad\cong
\{\beta\in\operatorname{Sur}(\Pi,Q'):
(q|_{Q'})\beta=\alpha_2\}.
\end{aligned}
\tag{92.7}
\]

If \(\theta:G\to E\) is an elementary scalar decoration, it kills the odd group \(N\) and factors through \(\bar\theta:Q\to E\). Equation (92.7) then refines to
\[
\begin{aligned}
&\{f:
f_{\mathrm{tame}}=\alpha,\ 
\theta f=\psi,\ 
\operatorname{im}f=H\times_CQ'\}\\
&\qquad\cong
\{\beta\in\operatorname{Sur}(\Pi,Q'):
(q|_{Q'})\beta=\alpha_2,\ 
(\bar\theta|_{Q'})\beta=\psi\}.
\end{aligned}
\tag{92.8}
\]

The right side is literally identical for \(\Gamma_A\) and \(G_{\mathbb Q_2}\), not merely equinumerous.

Taking \(Q'=Q\) recovers Turn 91’s epimorphism bijection. Taking all proper \(Q'\) simultaneously shows that every proper-image contribution, every marked subgroup, every scalar decoration, and every Möbius subtraction in the terminal branch is already encoded in the common pro-\(2\) quotient.

The terminal scalar branch is therefore not just a base case with the correct total count. Its complete fixed-frame image theory is the compatible subgroup theory of the finite \(2\)-group \(Q\).

**ESTABLISHED**: Strengthened the terminal scalar theorem from an epimorphism criterion to an exact image-poset equivalence. Every subgroup of \(G=H\times_{H_2}Q\) projecting onto the fixed tame quotient is uniquely \(H\times_{H_2}Q'\) for a compatible subgroup \(Q'\le Q\), and every fixed-frame decorated map has image \(H\times_{H_2}\operatorname{im}\beta\). Thus all proper-image corrections in the terminal branch reduce bijectively to the common maximal pro-\(2\) quotient.

**GAP**: The private 5402-group verifier remains unrun. The nonterminal proof still rests on ordinary Thompson criticality and the finite-Heisenberg and Wall–Arf/local-Kummer propositions consolidated in Turn 89.

**NEXT**: Insert (92.1)–(92.8) as the complete induction base in the fixed-frame theorem, replacing both the generation clause and every terminal proper-image subtraction by the exact compatible-\(2\)-subgroup formula.

[BREAKTHROUGH]
